candidates.title: Petition signature counting resumes as Alberta separatist group gets partial win at court of appeal
candidates.description: An Alberta separatist group has secured a partial win at the province’s top court after a lower court judge quashed its referendum petition last month.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Apartments can become sweltering in summer. Why heat bylaws could be coming to a city near you
candidates.description: As temperatures and deaths connected to heat keep climbing, more tenants, politicians and climate advocates are calling for cooling rules. During municipal debates — from councils in British Columbia to Newfoundland — the same question keeps coming up: Who should pay for the upgrades?
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Family of teen fatally injured on Quebec ski lift grieves a life that 'shouldn't be over'
candidates.description: Megan Bell died in February, four days after a school field trip to Centre Vorlage in western Quebec. According to police, her clothes got caught in a chairlift. For the first time, her parents are speaking out about that life-changing tragedy, and the questions they have about the emergency response that day.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: 'Monster' tornado destroys century-old brick farmhouse in western Manitoba
candidates.description: Severe thunderstorms on Sunday swept through beleaguered western Manitoba — still recovering from storms earlier this month — bringing at least one tornado that a storm chaser called a "monster."
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: High heat and humidity forecast for southern Quebec and Ontario
candidates.description: Environment Canada is warning about an extended period of hot and humid weather forecast for southern Quebec and Ontario this week.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Akwesasne man pleads guilty in smuggling case that left family with 2 toddlers dead
candidates.description: A 31-year-old man pleaded guilty last week for his role in a human smuggling conspiracy that led to the 2023 drowning deaths of a family, including two children under the age of three, in the St. Lawrence River.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: First wildfire evacuees from Fort Simpson, N.W.T., arrive in Yellowknife
candidates.description: Firefighters continue to tackle an out-of-control fire on the outskirts of the Dehcho community, as many of Fort Simpson's 1,300 residents are evacuating to Yellowknife.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Search underway for convicted killer believed to have escaped from Vancouver Island prison
candidates.description: A search is underway for a convicted killer police describe as "dangerous," who is believed to have escaped from a federal prison on Vancouver Island.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Green home retrofit program expands to 4 provinces, renters now eligible
candidates.description: The federal government is expanding a greener homes retrofit program to Nova Scotia, Prince Edward Island, Quebec and British Columbia.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Tom Siddon, cabinet minister during Oka Crisis, dies at 84
candidates.description: Tom Siddon, who served as a cabinet minister under Prime Minister Brian Mulroney during the Oka crisis and later helped to establish Nunavut, has died at 84.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: NBA star Gilgeous-Alexander headlines Canada roster ahead of FIBA Americas qualifiers
candidates.description: Two-time NBA MVP ShaiGilgeous-Alexander was the biggest name added to Canada's roster for the upcoming FIBA Men's Basketball World Cup 2027 Americas Qualifiers.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Northern Manitoba evacuations continue Sunday as wildfire threat grows
candidates.description: Hundreds of people have been forced out of their homes as wildfires continue to threaten communities in northern Manitoba.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: A glacial lake in B.C. is at risk of bursting — again. How climate change has raised this risk
candidates.description: The threat of a glacial lake overflowing and potentially sending a torrent of meltwater and debris rushing toward homes has become an annual fear for people living near British Columbia's Place Glacier. But experts says this is an increasing risk as the effects of human-caused climate change hit Canada's glaciers hard.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Wildfire near endangered whooping crane nests sparks scientific opportunity
candidates.description: It's too soon to say what the long-term impacts of earlier wildfires might be on the world’s only natural wild migratory whooping crane flock. But scientists could be a step closer to finding out after a wildfire in Wood Buffalo National Park came near a handful of the endangered species' nests.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Some Venezuelans fear a dark and dangerous future in aftermath of twin earthquakes
candidates.description: The future seems dark and dangerous for some Venezuelans amid the dust, debris and death shrouding parts of the country that suffered the brunt of twin earthquakes, creating one of the worst natural disasters in its history.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Italy, Balkans latest in Europe to feel brunt of record-breaking heat wave
candidates.description: Italy and the Balkans felt the impact on Monday of a record-breaking heat wave that has caused hundreds of excess deaths and disrupted daily life across the continent for more than a week, with growing concerns over the ‌spread of wildfires.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Inside the World Cup's international police command centre
candidates.description: CBC News got rare access inside the International Police Cooperation Center in Virginia, where law enforcement agencies from around the world are coordinating security for the FIFA World Cup, including monitoring drone threats and sharing intelligence.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. Supreme Court rejects Trump-led challenge against mail-in ballots
candidates.description: The U.S. Supreme Court on Monday ruled that states can count ballots that arrive after election day, a persistent target of President Donald Trump.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: As winds of change blow toward Cuba, how will Havana chart a path forward?
candidates.description: Six months into a tumultuous year for Cuba, the winds of change are aggressively blowing across the Straits of Florida, toward the island nation.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. Supreme Court declines Trump's appeal in E. Jean Carroll case
candidates.description: The U.S. Supreme Court declined on Monday to hear President Donald Trump's bid to overturn a $5-million US verdict in favor of E. Jean Carroll.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: U.S. Supreme Court rules against Trump bid to fire Lisa Cook, Federal Reserve governor
candidates.description: The U.S. Supreme Court refused on Monday ​to let Donald Trump fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook as it stood firm to preserve the central bank's cherished independence against an unprecedented challenge by the Republican president.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Russian strikes across Ukraine kill at least 9, injure dozens
candidates.description: Russian attacks across Ukraine killed nine people and wounded dozens on Monday, authorities said, with strikes continuing into the afternoon as the death toll climbed.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Australia proposes increasing fines for social media companies in under-16 ban
candidates.description: Australia introduced new legislation in Parliament on Monday to strengthen enforcement of its under-16 ‌social media ban and give its internet regulator more power to pursue tech giants in court for non-compliance.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Former NBA players Malik Beasley, Ed Davis latest to be charged in gambling scandal
candidates.description: Ex-NBA players Malik Beasley and Ed Davis have been indicted in the latest round of charges in the government's gambling investigation, authorities said Monday.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: King Charles reveals his income tax for the 1st time, but questions remain over royal finances
candidates.description: There was much interest in the move by King Charles to reveal for the first time how much income tax he pays, but in ways, the numbers that emerged this past week add to the longstanding opaqueness around royal finances.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: France reports 1,000 additional deaths as extreme heat shatters European records
candidates.description: France saw around 1,000 additional deaths last week at the height of its record-smashing heat wave, the country's public health agency said Sunday, as the head of the World Health Organization (WHO) warned that Europe is now the fastest-warming continent and needs to do more to protect its citizens.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: French police accused of targeting Black and Arab men with discriminatory fines
candidates.description: According to a report released last week by Human Rights Watch, (RE)Claim and the Community Centre for Development and Solidarity, French police systematically target Black and Arab youth, conducting identity checks and issuing on-the-spot fines.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Kentucky governor says 4 dead amid flooding caused by heavy rains
candidates.description: Flash flood warnings were in effect Saturday for parts of Kentucky and Indiana amid heavy rainfall, according to the National Weather Service.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Skydiving plane crashes in France, killing 11 people, officials say
candidates.description: The plane belonged to a parachutist school. The pilot and all 10 passengers — five students and five instructors — died in the accident, the prefecture said.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Tariff deal with Trump unlikely before U.S. midterms, says Canada's former trade chief
candidates.description: Canada's former chief trade negotiator says he doesn't expect Ottawa to reach a tariff deal with Washington before the U.S. midterm elections.
candidates.author: CBC News

candidates.title: Aftershock frays nerves as many Venezuelans left to fend for themselves
candidates.description: In areas devastated by the twin earthquakes, people are using crowbars, pickaxes and their bare hands to try to reach survivors.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'No one move!': The agonising silence as Venezuela rescuers listen for survivors
candidates.description: With tens of thousands of people still believed missing, teams listen for signs of life beneath the rubble.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Six people shot dead at centre for mothers and children in Germany
candidates.description: The male suspect who has been arrested was in a custody dispute over his baby daughter, police say.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Trump's final appeal of E Jean Carroll sex abuse case rejected
candidates.description: The Supreme Court decision means the US president will now have to pay Carroll the $5m (£3.6m) in damages she had been awarded.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: US says it has agreed to 'stand down' after exchange of strikes with Iran
candidates.description: A series of strikes over the weekend saw the US and Iran accuse each other of violating the ceasefire agreement.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: South African leader warns anti-migrant protesters ahead of unofficial deadline
candidates.description: Thousands of people from other African countries have left South Africa ahead of Tuesday's deadline set by anti-migrant groups.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Osaka 'pays love and respect to Japan' in Wimbledon kimono
candidates.description: Naomi Osaka has long been renowned for dazzling crowds with her on-court outfits - but making that compatible with Wimbledon's all-white dress code required special inspiration.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Supreme Court blocks Trump's attempt to fire Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook
candidates.description: The decision, seen as a win for central bank independence, sends the fight over removal back to the lower courts.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'You are Canadian heroes' - the forgotten hosts making World Cup history
candidates.description: Canada are making history at this World Cup with a run that is likely to change the face of football in the country forever.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Teyana Taylor's tears and Lauryn Hill tribute steal show at BET Awards
candidates.description: Taylor had an emotional reaction to being presented with the Icon of the Year award by Janet Jackson.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Fitness brand founder and Olympian Les Mills dies aged 91
candidates.description: The Les Mills International brand is now known globally for choreographed workouts set to music, like Body Pump and Body Combat.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Indian journalists condemn 'denial' of voting and passport rights of prominent editor
candidates.description: Rajagopal has alleged that he has been unable to renew his passport after his name was deleted from the electoral rolls.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Biden blasts Trump 'vanity projects', calling him a 'loser' at fundraising event
candidates.description: The former US president took aim at his successor's projects in Washington DC, including a planned new White House ballroom.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Senior South African police officer survives assassination attempt
candidates.description: Deputy crime intelligence boss Maj-Gen Feroz Khan was due to appear before an inquiry into police corruption this week.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: First dinosaur bone from Antarctica found in a drawer
candidates.description: The fossil, collected in Antarctica in 1985, is part of the tail of a beast called a Titanosaur.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Eleven killed after plane carrying skydivers crashes in eastern France
candidates.description: The pilot and 10 passengers - including five first-time parachutists - died in the incident, local officials said.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Three firefighters die battling huge wildfires on Colorado-Utah border
candidates.description: A number of blazes have been raging across Utah and Colorado, leading both governors to declare states of emergency.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: US and Iran exchange strikes and accuse each other of violating ceasefire
candidates.description: Iran says it has launched retaliatory attacks at US infrastructure in Kuwait and Bahrain after the US said it hit multiple targets across Iran.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: How population decline is exposing Germany's old divides
candidates.description: Decades after the fall of the Berlin Wall, a population slump in the east lays bare the existing divisions
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Mum rescued from Venezuela rubble with newborn baby tells BBC how he helped her survive
candidates.description: The "miracle" rescue of 18-day-old baby Juan David is a symbol of hope in Venezuela after the devastating earthquakes.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Loved and loathed: The making of India's viral liver doctor
candidates.description: Dr Cyriac Abby Philips has built a large online following, but often clashes with India's traditional medicine specialists.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Bad Bunny lights up London with history-making stadium show
candidates.description: Pride and a party as the Puerto Rican becomes the first Latin artist to headline a UK stadium.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: 'Hoping beyond hope': Rescue efforts continue at collapsed La Guaira building
candidates.description: At least 1,700 people are known to have been killed in the disaster but hope remains that others can continue to be found alive.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: BBC at Venezuela baseball stadium sheltering earthquake survivors
candidates.description: The survivors are hopeful the authorities will find them some form of temporary accommodation while the country rebuilds - for now they are dependant on donated food and clothing.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Anger directed at Venezuela's official response to earthquake recovery
candidates.description: Residents in quake-affected areas have expressed anger over the state's efforts to locate survivors and help victims, the BBC's Orla Guerin reports.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Watch: Inside the Venezuela country club now a makeshift hospital
candidates.description: Survivors of Venezuela's devastating earthquakes are being cared for at a makeshift hospital formed on the site of a country club in Caraballeda.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Tech firms are blaming AI for mega device and console price rises
candidates.description: Xbox consoles, Nintendo's new Switch 2 and Valve's Steam Deck are just some of the gadgets seeing price hikes in recent months.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Keep an eye out for tonight's Strawberry Moon
candidates.description: The Strawberry Moon is set to rise in UK skies tonight, marking the first full Moon of the summer season. It will reach its peak in the early hours of Tuesday, 30 June, according to the Royal Observatory Greenwich.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: I hear my son crying beneath the rubble, says Venezuela earthquake survivor
candidates.description: Andreina Valerio says she fears for her son, who is trapped with her partner and in-laws beneath a collapsed building.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Could legal 'personhood' help save Lough Neagh?
candidates.description: Rights of Nature could give Lough Neagh the same status as a person, entitled to the same protections.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Rescued bears cleared for 5,500-mile Suffolk flight
candidates.description: Vets say the bears from a former South Korean bile farm are ready to be moved to their forever home.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Where is best to see the Strawberry Moon tonight?
candidates.description: The first full Moon of the summer will be visible tonight, reaching its peak in the early hours of Tuesday.
candidates.author: BBC News

candidates.title: Pakistani airstrikes kill 36 civilians in Afghanistan and wound 160, officials say
candidates.description: Pakistan says it targeted militant hideouts along the Pakistan-Afghanistan border in response to militant attacks across Pakistan. Afghanistan condemned the strikes as acts of aggression.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. and Iran exchange fire despite ceasefire. And, Trump nominates a new head of ICE
candidates.description: The U.S. and Iran exchanged strikes over the weekend, putting their latest ceasefire in jeopardy. And, Trump has nominated a former Oklahoma state trooper to head Immigration and Customs Enforcement.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trump says the U.S. and Iran will meet in Qatar after weekend attacks
candidates.description: President Trump said talks with Iran would resume Tuesday in Qatar, despite the two sides trading attacks in the Gulf over the weekend. Iran did not confirm whether it will participate.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Former Iran nuclear deal negotiator on what U.S.-Iran 'new normal' looks like
candidates.description: NPR's Leila Fadel asks former national security adviser and 2015 Iran deal negotiator Jake Sullivan about ceasefire talks amid new strikes by the U.S. and Iran.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Could neo-Nazi youth, or 'active clubs,' have played a role in Belfast riots?
candidates.description: In the wake of racist mob violence in Northern Ireland, there has been attention on "active clubs," a global network of fascist, white nationalist youth groups that center activities around mixed martial arts.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Israel moves to formally recognize Armenian WWI deaths as a genocide
candidates.description: Israel's Cabinet approved a proposal to designate violence against Armenians by the Ottoman Empire during World War I as a genocide. The step reflects deteriorating ties between Israel and Turkey.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Australia to double potential fines over child social media accounts
candidates.description: Australia plans to double potential fines for social media platforms, including Facebook and Instagram, who fail to prevent Australian children from holding accounts.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: In Venezuela, silence has become a rescue tool
candidates.description: In Venezuela rescue crews now stop almost everything and ask for silence so they can hear anyone still alive underneath the rubble. NPR's Eyder Peralta reports from the port city of La Guaira.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: U.S. and Iran exchange strikes, underscoring the fragility of the ceasefire
candidates.description: President Trump accused Iran of violating their framework agreement, and Tehran threatened to halt negotiations with the U.S. entirely. Meanwhile, violence continued between Israel and Hezbollah.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Trade tensions shake up Brazil's caipirinha spirit
candidates.description: U.S. tariff pressure is pushing Europe and Brazil closer—opening new global doors for everything from aircraft parts to Brazil's cachaça, the base of the caipirinha.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Supreme Court cements Trump's power over agencies long considered independent
candidates.description: In a 6-3 decision, the Supreme Court struck down a 91-year-old precedent that has prevented presidents from removing members of independent agencies meant to be a check on his power.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Red, white and glowing blue: Trump's push for new reactors reaches the finish line
candidates.description: A program initiated by the Trump administration has allowed small companies to rush their testing of several new nuclear reactor designs. Some worry that safety is being compromised.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Sing-along time at a memory cafe is a highlight for people dealing with dementia
candidates.description: Music tends to stay with people even as dementia claims other memories. In Vermont, Memory Cafes provide communities for both dementia patients and their caregivers.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: They were world-class tennis rivals. Now friends, they've teamed up against cancer
candidates.description: They were the women's tennis champions of their generation. Now, Chris Evert and Martina Navratilova open up about friendship, cancer and retirement in the documentary Chris & Martina: The Final Set .
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: People are taking allergy and heartburn pills for PMS. Could it work?
candidates.description: Can the mix really help with premenstrual woes? Theoretically, the link between histamine and hormones could explain why the trendy DIY treatment seems to help some women.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: These church members disagree on politics. Together they're wiping out medical debt
candidates.description: Trinity Moravian Church, a politically diverse congregation in Winston-Salem, N. C., has been raising money to retire medical debt in the surrounding community.
candidates.author: NPR

candidates.title: Special Marine Warning issued June 29 at 4:45PM CDT until June 29 at 5:00PM CDT by NWS Duluth MN
candidates.description: For the following areas... Chequamegon Bay-Bayfield to Oak Point WI... Lake Superior west of a line from Saxon Harbor WI to Grand Portage MN beyond 5NM... Oak Point to Saxon Harbor WI... Outer Apostle Islands Beyond 5 NM from Mainland... Sand Island to Bayfield WI... At 444 PM CDT, a strong thunderstorm was located over Oak Point, moving east at 35 knots. HAZARD...Wind gusts to 40 knots and small hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Small craft could be damaged in briefly higher winds and suddenly higher waves. Locations impacted include... Ashland Breakwater Lighthouse, Chequamegon Bay, Basswood Island, Marble Point, Oak Point, Saxon Harbor, Madeline Island, La Pointe, Michigan Island, and Apostle Islands National Lake Shore.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Beach Hazards Statement issued June 29 at 4:45PM CDT until July 1 at 4:00AM CDT by NWS Green Bay WI
candidates.description: * WHAT...High waves and dangerous currents expected. * WHERE...Beaches along Lake Michigan in Door, Kewaunee and Manitowoc Counties. * WHEN...Through late Tuesday night. * IMPACTS...Dangerous swimming conditions are expected. Strong currents can pull swimmers into deeper water and high waves can sweep people off piers. * ADDITIONAL DETAILS...Several beaches that will be most susceptible to the dangerous swimming conditions include... Rock Island State Beach, Baileys Harbor beaches and Whitefish Dunes Beach in Door County. Cresent Beach and City of Kewaunee Beach in Kewaunee County. Point Beach, Neshotah Beach and Red Arrow Beach in Manitowoc County.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Small Craft Advisory issued June 29 at 4:44PM CDT until July 1 at 4:00AM CDT by NWS Green Bay WI
candidates.description: * WHAT...For the Dense Fog Advisory, dense fog will reduce visibility to under 1 mile at times. For the Small Craft Advisory, south winds 10 to 20 kts with gusts up to 25 kts and waves 4 to 7 ft. * WHERE...Lake Michigan nearshore waters from Washington Island to Sheboygan. * WHEN...For the Small Craft Advisory, until 4 AM CDT Wednesday. For the Dense Fog Advisory, until 10 AM CDT Tuesday. * IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft. Low visibility will make navigation difficult.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Dense Fog Advisory issued June 29 at 4:44PM CDT until June 30 at 10:00AM CDT by NWS Green Bay WI
candidates.description: * WHAT...For the Dense Fog Advisory, dense fog will reduce visibility to under 1 mile at times. For the Small Craft Advisory, south winds 10 to 20 kts with gusts up to 25 kts and waves 4 to 7 ft. * WHERE...Lake Michigan nearshore waters from Washington Island to Sheboygan. * WHEN...For the Small Craft Advisory, until 4 AM CDT Wednesday. For the Dense Fog Advisory, until 10 AM CDT Tuesday. * IMPACTS...Conditions will be hazardous to small craft. Low visibility will make navigation difficult.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued June 29 at 4:44PM CDT until June 29 at 5:00PM CDT by NWS Bismarck ND
candidates.description: At 444 PM CDT, a severe thunderstorm was located 11 miles northwest of Benedict, or 19 miles south of Minot, moving northeast at 40 mph. HAZARD...Golf ball size hail and 60 mph wind gusts. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...People and animals outdoors will be injured. Expect hail damage to roofs, siding, windows, and vehicles. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. This severe thunderstorm will be near... Sawyer and Velva around 455 PM CDT. Other locations impacted by the severe thunderstorm include Logan.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued June 29 at 5:43PM EDT until June 29 at 6:45PM EDT by NWS Greenville-Spartanburg SC
candidates.description: SVRGSP The National Weather Service in Greenville-Spartanburg has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Northwestern Stephens County in northeastern Georgia... Habersham County in northeastern Georgia... * Until 645 PM EDT. * At 542 PM EDT, a severe thunderstorm was located 7 miles northwest of Toccoa, or 4 miles south of Tallulah Falls, moving southwest at 15 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and penny size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect damage to trees and power lines. * Locations impacted include... Clarkesville, Cornelia, Demorest, Mount Airy, Tallulah Falls, Hollywood, Turnerville, Baldwin, and Tallulah Gorge State Park.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued June 29 at 5:43PM EDT until June 29 at 6:30PM EDT by NWS Tampa Bay Ruskin FL
candidates.description: SVRTBW The National Weather Service in Ruskin has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Northeastern Sumter County in central Florida... * Until 630 PM EDT. * At 543 PM EDT, a severe thunderstorm was located over Wildwood, and is nearly stationary. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and quarter size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... Wildwood, Lake Panasoffkee, Coleman, and Adamsville.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued June 29 at 5:43PM EDT until June 29 at 6:30PM EDT by NWS Marquette MI
candidates.description: SVRMQT The National Weather Service in Marquette has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Houghton County in western Upper Michigan... Northwestern Baraga County in north central Upper Michigan... Northeastern Ontonagon County in western Upper Michigan... * Until 630 PM EDT. * At 543 PM EDT, severe thunderstorms were located along a line extending from 9 miles west of Redridge to Mass City, moving east at 65 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and penny size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Expect damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... Houghton, Calumet, Hancock, Laurium, Chassell, Lake Linden, South Range, Redridge, Houghton County Airport, The Lower Entrance of Portage Canal, Dollar Bay, and Hubbell.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued June 29 at 4:43PM CDT until June 29 at 5:45PM CDT by NWS Bismarck ND
candidates.description: SVRBIS The National Weather Service in Bismarck has issued a * Severe Thunderstorm Warning for... Northeastern Burleigh County in south central North Dakota... Northwestern Kidder County in south central North Dakota... * Until 545 PM CDT. * At 443 PM CDT, a severe thunderstorm was located near Wing, or 35 miles northeast of Bismarck, moving northeast at 30 mph. HAZARD...Golf ball size hail and 60 mph wind gusts. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...People and animals outdoors will be injured. Expect hail damage to roofs, siding, windows, and vehicles. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. * Locations impacted include... Wing, Tuttle, Robinson, Arena and Horsehead Lake.
candidates.author: National Weather Service

candidates.title: Severe Thunderstorm Warning issued June 29 at 4:42PM CDT until June 29 at 5:00PM CDT by NWS Duluth MN
candidates.description: At 442 PM CDT, severe thunderstorms were located along a line extending from near Red Cliff to near Washburn to near Sanborn, moving east at 40 mph. HAZARD...60 mph wind gusts and half dollar size hail. SOURCE...Radar indicated. IMPACT...Hail damage to vehicles is expected. Expect wind damage to roofs, siding, and trees. These severe storms will be near... Ashland, Bayfield, La Pointe, Odanah, and Madeline Island around 445 PM CDT. Other locations in the path of these severe thunderstorms include Big Bay State Park.
candidates.author: National Weather Service
